spec_match.rationale: The page now uses the Chrome 148 declarative syntax, probes shadowrootadoptedstylesheets specifier lists, and explains when the constructed stylesheet path is a fallback simulation.

interactive.rationale: Tabs, component-count ranges, specifier selection, and the runtime probe all update visible output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still focuses on shared CSSStyleSheet identity versus duplicated inline styles and now ties that comparison directly to declarative module adoption.

edge_cases.rationale: The probe includes valid duplicate specifiers, split specifiers, and a missing-specifier fallback path with explicit readout.

references.rationale: A labelled references section now includes chromestatus.com/feature/4790543041298432, the WHATWG pull request, and the explainer.

design_system.rationale: New controls use the shared CSS variables and button background overrides include explicit text colors.

summary: The style-isolation demo now includes the required ChromeStatus link and an interactive adoption probe that covers supported, simulated fallback, and missing-specifier behavior.
